bot.name: Support Bot
bot.description: Rule-based support assistant for SecureMsg. Answers only based on implemented features.

behavior.unsupported_feature_response: This feature is not supported in SecureMsg at the moment.

fallbacks.empty_input: Tell me what you need help with, and I’ll guide you step by step.
fallbacks.unknown: I can help with security, groups, messages, files, unread badges, and account issues. What problem are you facing?
fallbacks.low_confidence: Can you clarify what you are trying to do or which feature you are asking about?

blocked_requests: encryption key, salt, iv, or secret disclosure
safety.refusal_template: I can’t help with that request. I can explain supported SecureMsg features and safe troubleshooting steps.

examples: Can the server read my messages?

reply.short: Messages are encrypted client-side using AES-256-GCM. The server stores only encrypted payloads.
reply.detailed: SecureMsg encrypts all message content in the client using AES-256-GCM with per-message randomness. Session passwords and keys are kept in memory only and are never stored on the server.

examples: Can a non-member see group chat?

reply.short: Only group members or the owner can read and send messages. Only the owner can delete the group.
reply.detailed: Group access is enforced by database RLS policies. Messages are readable only by members or the owner, and deletion rights are restricted to the group owner.

examples: Can I send a private message?

reply.short: SecureMsg sends direct messages as encrypted payloads between two users.
reply.detailed: Before a message leaves your browser, it is encrypted with the chat session password. The server stores and delivers the encrypted content, IV, salt, sender, receiver, and timestamp, but not the readable message text.

examples: Why do I need to unlock chats again?

reply.short: Chats must be unlocked with the session password used for encryption.
reply.detailed: Session passwords and derived keys live only in memory. After refresh or logout, you must unlock chats again to decrypt messages.

examples: Why can’t I download a file?

reply.short: Files are encrypted before upload and require an unlocked session to download.
reply.detailed: Encrypted files use the same session-based encryption model as messages. If download fails, unlock the chat session and retry.

examples: Why did my unread count reset?

reply.short: Unread badges are session-based and reset after reload or logout.
reply.detailed: Unread counts are managed in frontend memory and are not yet persisted in the database.

examples: How do I delete my account?

reply.short: Check credentials and network status. Account deletion is permanent.
reply.detailed: SecureMsg uses Supabase authentication. Deleting your account permanently removes your data and messages.

examples: I get 42501 errors when sending messages

reply.short: This usually means a Supabase key or RLS policy mismatch.
reply.detailed: Verify service-role vs anon keys, reapply RLS policies from supabase.sql, restart the server, and re-login.

limitations: Support Bot is rule-based, not generative AI
Unread counts are not persisted across reloads
Encryption metadata is visible by design
